65 degrees C and 30% relative humidity (RH) under 340 nm UV light", "fact_category": "methodology", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["UV aging", "dark aging", "85 degrees C", "13.5% RH"]}, {"statement": "Deacetylation products catalyze interfacial hydrolysis reactions, creating interdependency between deacetylation and hydrolytic depolymerization.", "direct_quote": "deacetylation products catalyze interfacial hydrolysis reactions", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.9, "confidence": 0.92, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["deacetylation", "hydrolysis", "catalysis"]}, {"statement": "EVA at the glass interface degrades more than at the cell interface, explaining why this initially stronger interface becomes preferential for delamination.", "direct_quote": "EVA that interfaces with the glass as opposed to the cell experiences increased levels of degradation", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.87, "confidence": 0.92, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["glass/EVA interface", "delamination"]}, {"statement": "Under light conditions, EVA crystallinity increased by 5% over first 1000 hours, then dropped approximately 18% between 2000 and 5000 hours.", "direct_quote": "crystallinity increases by 5%. However, between 2000 and 5000 hours, the crystallinity drops by ~18%", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.78, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["crystallinity", "EVA", "DSC"]}, {"statement": "Elevated temperatures alone induced limited sodium ion migration across the glass/EVA interface.", "direct_quote": "elevated temperatures alone are shown to induce limited migration of sodium ions across the glass/EVA interface", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.72, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["sodium ion migration", "glass/EVA interface"]}, {"statement": "Broad -OH stretch peak at glass/EVA interface increased approximately 5x after 10,000 hours while EVA/cell interface remained negligible.", "direct_quote": "at the glass/EVA interface, we see a ~5x increase", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.75, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["FTIR-ATR", "OH stretch", "glass/EVA interface"]}], "evidence_summary": "Stanford/SLAC/NREL 2022 study on EVA degradation over module lifetime (10,000h = 15-20yr). 3-stage sequence: deacetylation -> beta-scission -> hydrolytic depolymerization.
